2606.09607.title: Closure-Validated Circuit Discovery in Attention Heads: Co-activation Proposes, Ablation Disposes
2606.09607.paper_summary: Interpretability increasingly treats groups of components, not individual units, as the basic object, and proposes to find them by clustering co-activation statistics. We ask whether such a cheap signal actually identifies an attention-head circuit. Adapting a sparse-autoencoder clustering recipe to attention heads -- but validating by causal ablation rather than reconstruction -- we cluster heads and then run a closure test: ablate the discovered community and compare per-example damage to matched-random controls. Across two dense 1B-scale models (Pythia 1B, OLMo 1B) and two input distributions, the communities pass closure. In a Mixture-of-Experts model (OLMoE-1B-7B), route-conditional clustering recovers a statistically real signal that nonetheless does not survive closure -- ablation improves loss, the wrong direction. Extending closure across training, attention-target selectivity and participation ratio decouple from function in both directions. We conclude that a cheap signal is a circuit proposal, not a confirmed circuit; closure is what separates them.
2606.09607.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋性越來越多地將組件群體視為基本對象，而非個別單位，並提議通過聚類共同激活統計來尋找它們。我們詢問這樣一個廉價信號是否實際上能識別一個注意力頭電路。將稀疏自編碼器聚類方法調整為注意力頭——但通過因果切除而非重建來驗證——我們聚類頭部，然後進行閉合測試：切除發現的社群，並將每個示例的損害與匹配隨機對照進行比較。在兩個密集的1B規模模型（Pythia 1B，OLMo 1B）和兩個輸入分佈中，這些社群通過了閉合測試。在一個專家混合模型（OLMoE-1B-7B）中，路由條件聚類恢復了一個統計上真實的信號，但仍然無法通過閉合——切除改善了損失，這是錯誤的方向。擴展閉合至訓練過程中，注意力目標選擇性和參與比率在功能上雙向解耦。我們得出結論，廉價信號是一個電路提議，而不是一個確認的電路；閉合是將它們區分開來的因素。
2606.09607.author: Yongzhong Xu et.al.
